_provenance: GROUNDED IN A COMMITTED, REPRODUCIBLE RUN — server/data/edf_corpus/ holds the 8 spec_*.md and the full per-obligation dump (obligations.json) this breakdown is computed from, produced by pilot/A-proof-carrying-ui/edf_coverage.mjs (gemini-2.5-flash, transparent rule-based classifier). Re-running regenerates specs stochastically and lands near, not exactly on, these counts; this file records ONE committed run. lib/coverage.mjs asserts internal consistency at boot.

named.note: largest share of under-specified / subjective effects

perSpec.note: All 8 specs of the committed corpus are named with their exact coverage; the spec_*.md and obligations.json are in server/data/edf_corpus/ for audit.

theoremMap.note: Residual kinds correspond to Theorem 1 case (c) — non-observable / under-determined effects (p not in L). Infinite-universal obligations (case (b)) did not arise in this 8-spec sample.

caveat: Rule-based first pass. No human-labeled ground truth and no inter-annotator-agreement study yet. Read 82.4% as the output of a transparent, auditable heuristic for sizing the certifiable fragment — not a validated measurement. Coverage is generator-dependent: the same classifier over gemini-3.5-flash specs yields ~99% (stronger generators emit fewer under-specified obligations), so the ~18% residual here is a property of this corpus, not a fixed constant. A camera-ready needs human labels + IAA (e.g. Cohen's kappa) over the obligations.
